wander_scroll.title: Find The Wanderer's Field Notes scroll (drops from wandering traders, abandoned campsites, road loot)

subtitle.text: The Atlas fills as you walk. Whoever finds it after you will know where you went.

description: CH.01 · THE WANDERER'S FIELD NOTES
▸ The Antique Atlas is one of the most important early crafts.
It auto-records every biome you walk through.
It places icons for structures it detects.
It never runs out of paper.
◈ ANTIQUE ATLAS FEATURES
└ Auto-maps biomes as you explore
└ Detects and marks structures automatically
└ Supports custom markers (place by hand)
└ Works across dimensions (separate Nether/End maps)
└ Shares map data with other players if given same atlas
◈ SCROLL: The Wanderer's Field Notes
└ Found in: wandering traders, campsite ruins,
road loot tables, first village chests
└ Research time: instant (1 sec)
└ Unlocks: cartography progression awareness
▸ The Cartographer's Mark scroll (Ch02) goes deeper.
The Wanderer's Notes is the introduction:
observe the world, write it down, give it to someone else.
▸ Scroll usage parallel:
Scrolls ARE the Wanderer's Field Notes of the modpack.
Someone learned it. They wrote it down.
You're reading it now.
▶ Find the Wanderer's Field Notes scroll [check]
▶ Craft an Antique Atlas [check]
◇ The Antique Atlas saves its data to the world file.
It survives death. Your map is permanent.
